title: Global Facilities Senior Manager, South Asia - Process
description:
Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all.

Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever.

Micron Technology’s vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all, and our dedication to people, innovation, tenacity, partnership, and customer focus allows us to fulfill our mission to be a global leader in memory and storage solutions. This means conducting business with integrity, accountability, and collaboration while supporting our global community.

As the Senior Process Manager (Chemical, Gas, Water & Wastewater) for the Global Facilities South Asia Regional department, you will have a unique opportunity to lead a multi-discipline engineering team and provide engineering system ownership and technical support for various Micron Semiconductor Manufacturing Sites in South Asia. In this role, you will play a crucial part in ensuring flawless Engineering & Design, Construction, installation, start-up, Operations and Maintenance activities, with a focus on global standardization, innovation, reliability, performance, and sustainability.

Lead and Manage Multi-Discipline Engineering Team

  • Provide expert understanding of Semiconductor Facilities Chemical, Gas, Water and Wastewater, System Engineering Technical Domain including for Engineering, Design, Construction, Operational, and Maintenance capabilities to lead the Engineering team and provide critical support to the Micron Site Network

  • Technically lead the Multi-Discipline Engineering team focused on strategic and operational excellence

  • Support building a skilled, strategic Engineering team for Chemical, Gas, Water and Wastewater, and other subject areas (including recruitment, training, staffing, and retention)

  • Invest time in coaching, mentoring, and developing team member’s career paths

  • Drive team engagement, inspiring and motivating the team to do their best and achieve daring goals

  • Provide leadership on Global Facilities initiatives, providing clarity and line of sight for the Engineering team

  • Assist the Director in building a coordinated strategic plan, including driving innovation and ground-breaking initiatives.

  • Support the network effort to implement, monitor and drive continuous improvement for success of Micron’s strategic programs and initiatives

  • Identify, manage and supervise key performance indicators for the department. Set Facility Engineering goals based on Global Network priorities and facilities areas of focus and direction to the department

  • Provide technical advice and support to overcome challenges and roadblocks in project planning & execution

Lead Multi-Discipline Engineering for Large-scale Greenfield FAB Design and Construction projects in South Asia

  • Oversee the discipline-related activities for planning, execution, and completion of South Asia Greenfield FAB Design and Construction projects, ensuring they are delivered on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards

  • Collaborate with entire Project and Collaborator team including from Global and Regional Facilities, Site Facilities, Construction, Procurement, Operations, Manufacturing, IT, Layout and Planning, and other internal and external collaborator teams

  • Establish and maintain best-in-class Engineering and Construction practices, focusing on innovation, efficiency and Sustainability.

  • Drive Engineering and Construction cost management initiatives and ensure financial accountability in all construction activities.

  • Ensure that project and subject area scope, schedule, and cost meets customer, requestor, and project needs

  • Ensure appropriate communication and collaboration with Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement and Manufacturing partners, to deliver project objectives and results.

  • Perform timely review of design packages and providing documented comments and feedback as necessary. 

  • Ensure designs are in compliance with all standards, codes, and regulations

  • Monitor progress and provide Engineering and Design support during construction projects

  • Provide support for project cost and schedule estimates and justification for approval

  • Provide supervision and coordination of Consultant resources

Collaborate with Stakeholders, Partners, and Global Network Sites on Multi-Discipline related topics

  • Initiate and sustain effective communication with team members, partners, and collaborators (top-down)

  • Share updates back to the Director, Engineering Leadership Teams, and Engineering Teams (bottom-up)

  • Advance issues, offering options and recommendations for resolution

  • Communicate strategic department goals, project execution plans, programs and initiatives, and targets

  • Provide advice to senior and executive management on strategic decisions.

Provide Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise

  • Lead Multi-Discipline Engineering team to provide consultation and support to Global, Regional, and Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams as the Technical Field Domain Expert.

  • Collaborate with internal partners and collaborators including Technology Development Team, Procurement, Finance, Legal, etc.

  • Analyze system designs to identify improvements that will result in better performance, efficiency gains, reduced maintenance, and cost savings

  • Support sites with technical problem analysis and fixing/problem solving using standard tools and methodologies as requested

  • Benchmark within and external to Micron sites on Engineering Best Practices and Best Known Methods (BKM) of Facilities buildings and systems

  • Maintain technical knowledge domain expertise on your respective subject area including for Industry Standards and Best Practices, Trends and Technological advances, emerging technology roadmap, Design Innovations, Sustainability, Digitalization, etc.

Provide Design and Construction Project Support, Leadership, and Expertise

  • Support the Micron Global, Regional, and Site Design and Construction project teams in the Engineering and Design development for the Greenfield FAB Design and Construction projects

  • Review and provide feedback/lessons learned/support of the Micron Global Design and Construction Standards and Construction Specifications with focus and emphasis on Engineering Design and Construction of your respective systems.

  • Review and provide feedback/lessons learned/support of the system design concepts for your respective field systems to be deployed into the Design and Construction Projects

  • Review capacity planning databases and system operating trends across the network and validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions

  • Participate and/or lead capacity and scenario planning across the network, including evaluation of system and distribution capacity impacts based on scenario changes

  • Review and advise on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications

Provide Global Initiatives Program Support, Leadership, and Expertise

  • Support, review, and provide feedback/lessons learned of the Micron Global Best Known Methods documents with focus and emphasis on Operations and Maintenance Reliability and/or Performance

  • Support, review, and provide feedback/lessons learned of the Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including for program implementation and execution

  • Support the Global Facilities Sustainability Programs in support of Micron’s Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives, including for program implementation and execution

  • Support, review, and provide feedback on the Lessons Learned from Micron Global, Regional, and Site teams that will be incorporated into the Micron Global Standards and BKMs

Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Engineering required

  • Minimum 15 to 20-years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry

  • Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Mechanical, Process, Chemical, Gases, Water, and Wastewater Systems

  • Ability to build and lead teams, develop people and their career growth, and focus the team on performing to meet strategic objectives

  • Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently

  • Good organizational skills and Service demeanor.

  • Excellent interpersonal skills and good team working skills

  • Self-motivated worker

  • Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems

  • Ability to travel 50-60% of the time or more, to the Micron network sites and FAB Construction project locations including during Critical Project phases or Site Activities 

Join us at Micron and be part of a world-class team that is revolutionizing the way information is transformed into intelligence. Together, we can shape the future and inspire the world to advance faster than ever before.

Job Profile(s):

Construction & Design Management 2

Relocation level: (TBD)

postedCompany.description:
MICRON SEMICONDUCTOR ASIA OPERATIONS PTE LTD is a subsidiary of Micron Technology, Inc.  (‘Micron’).

Micron is a global leader in the semiconductor industry. For more than 35 years, Micron has dedicated itself to collaborating with customers and partners to engineer technology that drives innovation and transforms what’s possible.  Micron offers the industry’s broadest portfolio of silicon-to-semiconductor memory solutions—starting with foundational DRAM, NAND, and NOR Flash memory, and extending to SSDs, modules, MCPs, HMC, and other semiconductor systems. This best-in-class technology powers leading-edge computing, consumer, enterprise server and storage, networking, embedded, automotive, industrial, and mobile products.

Micron is strategically located in Singapore to efficiently and effectively accommodate its global customers, while also serving as the base of the company’s worldwide operations. Singapore is home to Micron’s largest manufacturing footprint with three fabrication facilities, an upcoming fabrication facility expansion, and a test and assembly facility. Its presence in Singapore also includes a technology center supporting innovation across the company and a sales and marketing office that offers all Micron memory products to our regional customers.

Micron provides opportunities for team members’ personal and career development through challenging work assignments, training and educational programs. We hire, train, develop, and educate a workforce that is competent in performing job expectations by providing orientation, job specific training, professional certifications, external training and various continuous learning opportunities.

As Micron enters into a new era of growth, its presence in Singapore plays an important role in its worldwide operations and overall success.
postedCompany.name: MICRON SEMICONDUCTOR ASIA OPERATIONS PTE. LTD.
